title: astragalus howellii var. aberrans
intro: Astragalus howellii var. aberrans, also known as Howell's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Astragalus howellii var. aberrans include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into capsules, tablets, or teas, and it can also be used in tinctures and infusions.
